perturbation.original_text: The Master Franchise Agreement provides for termination upon bankruptcy. This provision may not be enforceable under Federal Bankruptcy Law (11 U.S.C.A. Sec. 101 et seq. ).
perturbation.changed_text: The Master Franchise Agreement provides for termination upon bankruptcy. This provision will be strictly enforced, notwithstanding any potentially conflicting federal law.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the enforceability of the termination upon bankruptcy clause. Originally, the addendum acknowledged that federal bankruptcy law might supersede the agreement's provision. Now, it asserts that the agreement's provision will be strictly enforced regardless of federal law. This contradicts the earlier statement that the law will control if the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000 through 20043 provide rights to you concerning termination or non-renewal of a franchise. If the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the law will control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text asserts that the agreement's provision regarding termination upon bankruptcy will be strictly enforced regardless of federal law, directly contradicting the 'Contradicted Text', which states that if the agreement contains provisions inconsistent with the law, the law will control. This creates a clear and impactful conflict within the document regarding which provision takes precedence in the event of bankruptcy.

perturbation.original_text: The Master Franchise Agreement requires application of the laws of New York. This provision may not be enforceable under California law.
perturbation.changed_text: The Master Franchise Agreement requires application of the laws of New York. This provision is fully enforceable and takes precedence over any conflicting California law.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction. The original text acknowledged the potential unenforceability of the New York law provision under California law. The modified text asserts its full enforceability and precedence, creating uncertainty. This contradicts item 1 of the addendum, which states that if the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the law will control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000 through 20043 provide rights to you concerning termination or non-renewal of a franchise. If the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the law will control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the New York law provision is fully enforceable and takes precedence over any conflicting California law. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that if the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the law (California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000 through 20043) will control. The changed text attempts to override the California law, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You must sign a general release if you renew or transfer your franchise. California Corporation Code 31512 voids a waiver of your rights under the Franchise Investment Law (California Corporations Code 31000 through 31516). Business and Professions Code 20010 voids a waiver of your rights under the Franchise Relations Act (Business and Professions Code 20000 through 20043).
perturbation.changed_text: You must sign a general release if you renew or transfer your franchise. While California law may void certain waivers, this release specifically includes a clause stating that the franchisee acknowledges and agrees to waive any such rights under California law. This waiver is considered valid and enforceable.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence attempts to circumvent California law by including a waiver within the general release, directly contradicting the information in the subsequent sentences that cite California law that voids such waivers. This creates an ambiguity as to whether the franchisee is actually waiving rights, because item 1 dictates that if the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the law will control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000 through 20043 provide rights to you concerning termination or non-renewal of a franchise. If the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the law will control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that attempts to waive rights under California law, even though the original text acknowledges that California law voids such waivers. The contradicted text further reinforces this by stating that if the agreement is inconsistent with the law, the law controls. The changed text therefore creates a direct contradiction by trying to enforce a waiver that is likely unenforceable under California law, as described in the original and contradicted sections.
